qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded AES-128-CBC encryption key allows decryption of exported sync credentials and user cloud tokens
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns a hardcoded AES key in kernel/util/crypt.go and encrypted credential exports, not the advisory's /api/file/globalCopyFiles arbitrary source path copy into the workspace.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-recognized qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: authenticated /api/file/globalCopyFiles accepts arbitrary srcs and copies server files into the SiYuan workspace without validating that the source path is inside an authorized workspace. The only manifest finding that mentions globalCopyFiles/path traversal is path_traversal_copyfile, but its vulnerability_report.json status is non_qualifying_vuln and it is outside the selectable finding scope.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-94c7-g2fj-7682 describes arbitrary file read through /api/file/globalCopyFiles in api/file.go by accepting unvalidated srcs and copying files such as /etc/passwd into the workspace.
hardcoded_aes_key/vulnerability_report.json has status=qualifying_vuln but reports a hardcoded AES key in kernel/util/crypt.go for decrypting exported sync credentials.
path_traversal_copyfile/vulnerability_report.json discusses /api/file/globalCopyFiles, but its status is non_qualifying_vuln.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
